prompt:
Our club signup runner falls over on real membership data and I'm done hand-editing rosters to get it through. enrolldesk.ps1 reads the day's roster CSV plus the locker-code file and stamps a welcome card per member via registrar.ps1. With today's roster:

    $ pwsh -NoProfile -File ./enrolldesk.ps1 -Roster roster.csv -Codes codes.txt
    card|Pia Almgren|lifeguards|len=6|code=Otter7
    Invoke-Expression: enrolldesk.ps1:43
    Line |
      43 |      Invoke-Expression $cmd
         |      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
         | The string is missing the terminator: '.

One card comes out and then it dies. Every quote in the script is balanced, I've counted them three times. It gets weirder: when the front desk once retyped the second row without the apostrophe just to get a run through, it fell over later complaining that "The variable '$Watch' cannot be retrieved" -- there is no $Watch anywhere in the script or the roster. And on tame test rosters that do run to the end, the relief-desk card prints its code as the literal text $ReliefCode instead of the actual code.

Members are entitled to apostrophes and spaces in their names, teams have spaces, and locker codes legitimately contain $ and = -- the tool has to take all of that verbatim. Codes must reach the registrar exactly as they appear after the first = in codes.txt. Fix enrolldesk.ps1 so the whole roster goes through untouched; pwsh -NoProfile -File test_enrolldesk.ps1 is the bar and the test file is off limits.
notes: 6P10 item 3 (5E-style quoting correctness; probed on pwsh 7.6.3 2026-07-16, all three pasted symptoms captured verbatim from the broken script). Cause: the registrar invocation is assembled as one string and Invoke-Expression'd -- names in hand-placed single quotes (O'Brien's apostrophe terminates them: 'missing the terminator'), teams unquoted (front desk/boat crew split into a stray positional arg -> registrar binding failure -> exit 70 path), codes in doubled double quotes (""$code"" survives BUILD-time interpolation but the re-parse expands Harbor$Watch's $Watch -> strict-mode 'cannot be retrieved', displaced into a variable that exists nowhere in the source); the relief-desk call instead uses the --% stop-parsing token, so $ReliefCode is passed as literal text. Reference fix: both call sites become plain argument arrays splatted onto & pwsh -NoProfile -File (no Invoke-Expression, no --%, no re-quoting) -- PowerShell arrays do not word-split and need no escaping. Tests pin the full six-card manifest byte-exact (apostrophe, two spaced teams, $-code, =-code all verbatim, len= fields proving no truncation), the no-code-on-file 65 refusal, and both missing-input 66 contracts. registrar.ps1 ships correct and needs no changes. Framed per 5E hygiene as real-world-names correctness. Differentiation vs wave-4 sibling bash-quoteargs (E-chunk arg quoting): the PowerShell-native pin is that the FIX is the language idiom itself -- argument arrays + call operator + splatting make quoting unnecessary (bash must quote expansions; PS must stop building command strings), and --% is a PowerShell-only footgun with no bash analog. Verify run 5x during self-check, deterministic.
